title: Composition of matter for use in organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: The present disclosure relates to compounds of Formula (I) or (II), as compounds capable of emitting delayed fluorescence, and uses of the compounds in organic light-emitting diodes.
claims:

An organic light-emitting diode (OLED) comprising an anode, a cathode, and at least one organic layer comprising a light-emitting layer between the anode and the cathode, wherein the light-emitting layer comprises: a host material; a compound of any one of claims 1-84; and a light-emitting material which is not a compound of any one of claims 1-84. 96. An organic light-emitting diode (OLED) comprising an anode, a cathode, and at least one organic layer comprising a light-emitting layer between the anode and the cathode, wherein the light-emitting layer comprises: a compound of any one of claims 1-84; and a light-emitting material which is not a compound of any one of claims 1-84. 97. A screen or a display comprising a compound of any one of claims 1-84. 98. A method of manufacturing an OLED display, the method comprising: forming a barrier layer on a base substrate of a mother panel; wherein said barrier layer comprises a plurality of cell panels; forming a plurality of display units in the plurality of cell panels on the barrier layer; forming an encapsulation layer on each of the display units; and applying an organic film to an interface portion between the cell panels; wherein the organic film comprises a compound of any one of claims 1-84.
